## 3dslibris 2.6.2

EPUB list and CSS margin fixes, battery indicator.

### New

- **Battery level indicator in the reader HUD**: the reader status bar now shows the current battery level next to the clock, in the format `~80%`, `~60%`, etc. A `+` prefix is shown while the console is charging (`+~80%`). Levels are approximate — the PTMU API in libctru exposes only six discrete steps (0 %, 20 %, 40 %, 60 %, 80 %, 100 %), so an exact percentage reading is not available on this hardware.

### Fixes & Improvements

- **`list-style-type: none` on `<ol>` and `<ul>` is now respected**: lists whose container element carries `list-style-type: none` via a CSS class or inline style no longer render bullets or numbered markers. Previously the suppression flag was only evaluated for the `<li>` element itself; the `<ol>`/`<ul>` container was skipped, so CSS class rules such as `ol.toc-list { list-style-type: none; }` had no effect. See [#76](https://github.com/RigleGit/3dslibris/issues/76).
- **CSS `margin` shorthand with `auto` values no longer drops adjacent length values**: shorthand rules such as `margin: auto 5%` or `margin: 0 auto` now correctly parse the non-`auto` tokens. Previously the parser stopped at the first `auto` keyword, discarding any percentage or pixel values that followed it. See [#35](https://github.com/RigleGit/3dslibris/issues/35).
- **Reduced heap allocations during pagination**: the shaped-glyph buffer used when measuring each text token is now reused across the whole pagination pass instead of being allocated and freed once per token. On large books this eliminates thousands of small allocations per open.
- **Button label draw code simplified**: extracted shared RTL right-alignment logic in the button renderer, removing three copies of the same inline measurement loop.
- **`ContainsRTL` simplified in BiDi utils**: extracted `IsRtlCodepoint` as a file-local helper and replaced 5 separate range checks with a single call, reducing per-token overhead during pagination.
- **Reuse BiDi analysis buffers across pagination**: added `bidi_cps` and `bidi_runs` fields to `parsedata_t` and passed them to `ShapeTextRunBidi`, so the codepoint and run vectors are reused across the whole pagination pass instead of allocated per token.
- **EPUB first-time cover extraction avoids one full temporary buffer copy**: the cover decode path now reuses the ZIP entry buffer instead of cloning it before format detection and fallback handling, lowering peak memory and shaving some overhead from first-time cover generation.
- **Large EPUB PNG/JPEG covers keep their thumbnail fast-path under the RGB safety guard**: the direct `libpng` and MuPDF thumbnail decoders now get a chance to produce the final `RGB565` cover before the fallback full-RGB decode limit rejects oversized source images.
- **Visible cover warmup starts sooner on New 3DS in conservative browser mode**: when the app stays in the current stability-first browser path, non-selected visible books on `new3DS` no longer wait as long before their first cover warmup jobs become eligible.
- **Metadata-only EPUB indexing in the browser stops carrying full OPF baggage**: the OPF parser now stops once the package metadata and cover candidates are known, and metadata-only indexing no longer retains manifest/spine entries that are irrelevant to title/author/cover discovery.
- **Tiny CSS font sizes are clamped to a readable range**: nested EPUB rules such as `font-size: small` inside another `font-size: small` container no longer shrink text below a usable size. Inline CSS font sizes are now bounded relative to the reader font size, matching the existing protection against oversized text.
- **EPUB text shaping avoids extra work on simple LTR text**: the layout path now skips a redundant RTL/Arabic scan after shaping and uses a direct ASCII fast path for common text fragments, reducing per-fragment overhead during first-time EPUB pagination.
- **EPUB content text is batched before shaping**: adjacent `chardata` callbacks are coalesced until the next element/style boundary, which reduces thousands of tiny shape operations during first-time pagination of large EPUB chapters.
- **No-op inline font-size changes are skipped**: when CSS resolves to the current font size, the parser no longer emits unnecessary `TEXT_FONT_SIZE` markers or matching restore markers.
- **CSS class lookups consolidated into a single pass per element**: the EPUB content parser previously made up to 18 separate map lookups per XML element — one for each CSS property (margins, font-size, text-align, page-break, float, clear, text-transform, etc.). These are now resolved in a single `LookupAllForClassAttr` pass, reducing per-element overhead by ~40% on element handling and shaving roughly 2.5 seconds off large book open times.

---

## ❤️ Community Shoutouts

3dslibris wouldn't be the same without your support! This version is dedicated to:

* **Issue reports and testing:** Thanks to everyone who reported and followed up on [#35](https://github.com/RigleGit/3dslibris/issues/35) and [#76](https://github.com/RigleGit/3dslibris/issues/76).

* **Fueling the Code:** A special thank you to my **Ko-fi supporters**. Your donations help keep the project going and keep me caffeinated!

*Want to support the project? Consider leaving a ⭐ on GitHub or [buying me a coffee](https://ko-fi.com/rigle)!*

### Included assets

- `3dslibris.cia`
- `3dslibris-debug.cia`
- `3dslibris.3dsx`
- `3dslibris-debug.3dsx`
- `3dslibris-sdmc.zip` (runtime files only; pair it with the `.3dsx` asset for Homebrew Launcher installs)
- `3dslibris-source.tar.gz`
